title: [HW] Write some "parameter design rationale" dox
body:
Maybe I'm not doing this correctly, but when I change[ this line ](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/d75874d194c1d5f1b731109b2a241d69b15ea4f4/test/Dialect/HW/modules.mlir#L74) to

```mlir
%r2 = hw.instance "inst3" @parameters<>(arg0: %a: i8) -> (out: i8)
```

I get:

```
/home/jodemme/circt/test/Dialect/HW/modules.mlir:74:9: error: 'hw.instance' op expected 2 parameters but had 0
  %r2 = hw.instance "inst3" @parameters<>(arg0: %a: i8) -> (out: i8)
        ^
/home/jodemme/circt/test/Dialect/HW/modules.mlir:74:9: note: see current operation: %2 = "hw.instance"(%arg0) {argNames = ["arg0"], instanceName = "inst3", moduleName = @parameters, parameters = [], resultNames = ["out"]} : (i8) -> i8
/home/jodemme/circt/test/Dialect/HW/modules.mlir:61:1: note: module declared here
hw.module @parameters<p1: i42 = 17, p2: i1>(%arg0: i8) -> (out: i8) {
```

Which implies that there's no way to use default parameter values...? Or is it expected that one inspect the module param decls for the defaults before constructing the instanceop?
